title: Trump Presidencies Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: Day 426 of Trump's second term: the USTR launched Section 301 investigations into manufacturing excess capacity across 15 countries on March 11, potentially laying groundwork for new tariffs. VP Vance heads a second DOGE task force in March 2026 with mixed economic results — unemployment rose to 4.4% with 92,000 jobs lost in February, while the federal deficit fell 19% year-over-year due to tariff revenue. Reports indicate DOGE cuts are hampering government operations during the ongoing Iran conflict. Trump has signed 249 executive orders in his second term through March 16, 2026.
